answer:
The recurrent laryngeal nerve innervates all intrinsic laryngeal muscles except the cricothyroid, which is supplied by the external branch of the superior laryngeal nerve. After recurrent laryngeal nerve damage, the cricothyroid remains functional, tensing the vocal fold, while the interarytenoids (adductors) are paralyzed, leaving the fold in an intermediate position. This matches option **F**.

\boxed{F}
